commit_msg: feat: build cinematic PyQt6 viewer — the 3-act haiku player
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Haiku Made Flesh
subtitle: He turned three lines into a window. Now he could watch himself watching.
when_where: 9:05 AM on the main branch, March 13th. Omkar's fingers still warm from coffee. The terminal glows with the kind of light that makes a man forget what day it is. PyQt6 sits waiting like a loaded gun.
who_whom: He built a stage for three-act haikus to play themselves out. The app would no longer just exist; it would perform. Every frame, every moment of his compressed poetry would be rendered cinematic, undeniable. He was both the director and the only audience member who mattered.
what_why: He coded a viewer that turned haiku into cinema—each three-line verse became a scene, a moment, a confession projected onto glass. The weapon wasn't the PyQt6 framework itself; it was the choice to make his internal monologue external, visible, undeniable. Now every haiku he wrote would be something he had to *watch* come alive. He'd trapped himself in his own gallery.
verdict: He didn't build a viewer to see the work. He built it so the work could see him back.
